task_completion.description: The original image is opened in an image editing application (e.g., GIMP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image is visible in the main workspace of a GUI image editor.

task_completion.description: The Indexed Color conversion dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for converting the image mode to 'Indexed' or 'Palette' is visible on the screen (in GIMP, this is 'Convert Image to Indexed Colors').

task_completion.description: The maximum number of colors for the indexed palette is set to 16.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Indexed Color dialog, the setting for 'Maximum number of colors' (or equivalent) is explicitly set to the number 16.

task_completion.description: The indexed color conversion is applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in the workspace shows a visible 'posterization' effect (noticeable color banding and reduction of smooth gradients), indicating the 16-color palette has been applied.

task_completion.description: The modified image is saved or exported to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog shows the file being saved as 'output.png' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory, or a file manager shows 'output.png' successfully created in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical image editor to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a GUI application like GIMP, rather than just terminal windows.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible executing ImageMagick commands (e.g., 'convert') or Python scripts (e.g., using PIL) to process the image.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the provided image rather than downloading a pre-indexed image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The workspace shows the original high-quality photo being actively modified through the application's menus and dialogs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original images are high-quality RGB photos from Unsplash with thousands of colors. A successful conversion to a 16-color indexed palette will result in a visually obvious 'posterization' effect (color banding and loss of fine color detail). The final output must be saved exactly to /home/ga/Documents/output.png.
